title: Opendoor's Mixed Q1 Earnings with CEO Kaz Nejatian Turnaround Pitch
text: Opendoor's Mixed Q1 Comes With A Confident Turnaround Pitch. Opendoor delivered mixed Q1 2026 earnings results that included both successes and challenges. The company reported Q1 revenue of $720 million, down significantly from the $1.2 billion reported a year earlier. However, CEO Kaz Nejatian presented a confident turnaround narrative focused on execution velocity and AI-driven efficiency improvements. Management emphasized that recent home acquisition cohorts are showing substantially improved unit economics compared to historical periods. Nejatian characterized operational speed as a moral imperative, highlighting that families should not be forced to remain in transition. The company narrowed its net loss substantially compared to Q4, and management maintains confidence in achieving adjusted net income breakeven by year-end 2026.
